title: Squadro games played in BGA
subtitle: Squadro games played in Board Game Arena with players' info and game record
description:
### Description

Game info of the [**Squadro**](https://en.gigamic.com/game/squadro) games played in [Board Game Arena](https://en.boardgamearena.com/). The dataset includes all necessary info to reproduce any game.

> **UPDATE**: More than **25,000** games registered!!!

### About _Squadro_
_Award-winning **perfect-information abstract game**_ where each player moves 5 pieces from one side of the board to the other and back. The first player to make a round trip with 4 of their pieces wins.

![Squadro](https://www.googleapis.com/download/storage/v1/b/kaggle-user-content/o/inbox%2F12551464%2F3dcfdf6b800dff85b8b2735a8a231296%2Fpic_jouet.jpg?generation=1682571958083859&alt=media)

### Dataset columns

- `id`: Game ID.
- `date`: Date the game ended.
- `winner`: Player who won the game.
- `loser`: Player who lost the game.
- `elo_winner`: [ELO](https://en.boardgamearena.com/faq?anchor=faq_account_elo) of the winner (after the game).
- `elo_loser`: [ELO](https://en.boardgamearena.com/faq?anchor=faq_account_elo) of the loser (after the game).
- `first_hand`: Player who performed the first move.
- `conceded`: Whether the game was completed or was conceded before completion.
- `record`: Record of all the game hands.

### How to interpret the `record` column?

- The `record` string names all hands played in the order they were performed.
- The picture below shows the nomenclature for each piece.
![Squadro coordinates reference](https://www.googleapis.com/download/storage/v1/b/kaggle-user-content/o/inbox%2F12551464%2F00d6657c9e11e31b22c38aaf5bb4bfb8%2Fsquadro.png?generation=1669106243786177&alt=media)

title: 02.Tic Tac Toe - Data Set
subtitle: Tic Tac Toe - Set of 1000+ games  with different algorithms
description:
This dataset represent more than 1000 + games of Tic Tac Toe played with different algorithms.

**Algorithms:**
- R - Random
- M - MinMax
- M-n - MinMax where the first n-move are random Ex: M-1 is a MinMax algorithm were the first move is random

**Combinations Example**
- RvsR - Random vs. Random 
- RvsM - Radom vs. MinMax 
- M-1vsM - MinMax -1 vs, MinMax

The dataset has 12 columns 1 for each turn (total 9) plus three for the outcome of the game. Each turn column has the player number (1 or 2) and the move X and Y coordinates. ex: [1|2|0], [2|1|1], ….

The last three columns hold the winner (if there is a winner) the game conclusion and the number of the final turn.

ex: Null, Draw, 8 or 1, H, 6. 
H stands for Horizontal victory V stands for Vertical victory D1 and D2 stand for Diagonal or Reverse Diagonal victories.

**Column Descriptions**
**Turn 1 to Turn 9**: Each turn is formatted as [player|x|y] — player number and move coordinates.
**Winner**: Player number who won the game, or Null if it was a draw.
**Conclusion**: Type of win:
- H: Horizontal
- V: Vertical
- D1: Diagonal
- D2: Reverse Diagonal
- Draw: No winner

**Final Turn**: Number of the last move played in the game.

title: Sea of Galilee & Dead Sea Levels
subtitle: Daily and Monthly Water Levels Respectively
description:
### Context

The Dataset is divided in 2:
- Daily water levels of Lake Kinneret (Sea of Galilee) 
- Monthly water levels of Dead Sea


### Content

The Sea of Galilee and Dead Sea are the two most important lakes in Israel. The Sea of Galilee or Kinneret in Hebrew, is the biggest body of fresh water above the surface in the country and therefore strategically very important. On the other hand the Dead Sea is a salt lake. This salinity makes for a harsh environment in which plants and animals cannot flourish, hence its name. Additionally is located at an astonishing 430m below sea level, making it the lowest land-based elevation on Earth.

Next, you can see a satellite image that includes both bodies of water. Interestingly enough you might be able to see that both of them are connected through the Jordan River. By nature water should flow from the Sea of Galilee to the Dead Sea through the Jordan River, but this is not usually the case since there is a dam that contains the water from flowing freely out of the Sea of Galilee. The purpose of this dam is to preserve the levels of the Sea of Galilee. Because of this, as you will see in the dataset, while the levels of the Sea of Galilee have been fluctuating over the past decades, the levels of the Dead Sea have just been decreasing, since it has been evaporating and not being replenished.




![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/7/7b/Dead_Sea_Galilee.jpg)
### Acknowledgements

Thanks to the Israeli government database, the data is obtained from their site.
Also thanks to @sudalairajkumar for his dataset "Chennai Water Management" and notebook for inspiring me to start this mini project.

### Inspiration

The idea is to see if we can use this dataset to visualize the trends of the levels of both bodies of water with time.

title: Ecommerce Dataset (Products & Sizes Included)
subtitle: this dataset has all features that are necessary for making a ecommerce website
description:
📦 Ecommerce Dataset (Products & Sizes Included)

🛍️ Essential Data for Building an Ecommerce Website & Analyzing Online Shopping Trends
📌 Overview
This dataset contains 1,000+ ecommerce products, including detailed information on pricing, ratings, product specifications, seller details, and more. It is designed to help data scientists, developers, and analysts build product recommendation systems, price prediction models, and sentiment analysis tools.

🔹 Dataset Features

Column Name	               Description
product_id	                       Unique identifier for the product
title	                                       Product name/title
product_description	       Detailed product description
rating	                               Average customer rating (0-5)
ratings_count	                       Number of ratings received
initial_price	                       Original product price
discount               	               Discount percentage (%)
final_price	                       Discounted price
currency	                               Currency of the price (e.g., USD, INR)
images	                               URL(s) of product images
delivery_options	               Available delivery methods (e.g., standard, express)
product_details	               Additional product attributes
breadcrumbs	                       Category path (e.g., Electronics > Smartphones)
product_specifications	       Technical specifications of the product
amount_of_stars	               Distribution of star ratings (1-5 stars)
what_customers_said	       Customer reviews (sentiments)
seller_name	                       Name of the product seller
sizes	                               Available sizes (for clothing, shoes, etc.)
videos	                               Product video links (if available)
seller_information	               Seller details, such as location and rating
variations	                       Different variants of the product (e.g., color, size)
best_offer	                       Best available deal for the product
more_offers	                       Other available deals/offers
category	                               Product category


📊 Potential Use Cases


📌 Build an Ecommerce Website: Use this dataset to design a functional online store with product listings, filtering, and sorting.
🔍 Price Prediction Models: Predict product prices based on features like ratings, category, and discount.
🎯 Recommendation Systems: Suggest products based on user preferences, rating trends, and customer feedback.
🗣 Sentiment Analysis: Analyze what_customers_said to understand customer satisfaction and product popularity.
📈 Market & Competitor Analysis: Track pricing trends, popular categories, and seller performance.
🔍 Why Use This Dataset?
✅ Rich Feature Set: Includes all necessary ecommerce attributes.
✅ Realistic Pricing & Rating Data: Useful for price analysis and recommendations.
✅ Multi-Purpose: Suitable for machine learning, web development, and data visualization.
✅ Structured Format: Easy-to-use CSV format for quick integration.

📂 Dataset Format
CSV file (ecommerce_dataset.csv)
1000+ samples
Multi-category coverage
🔗 How to Use?
Download the dataset from Kaggle.
Load it in Python using Pandas:
python
Copy
Edit
import pandas as pd  
df = pd.read_csv("ecommerce_dataset.csv")  
df.head()
Explore trends & patterns using visualization tools (Seaborn, Matplotlib).
Build models & applications based on the dataset!

title: Bitcoin Historical Data
subtitle: Bitcoin data at 1-min intervals from select exchanges, Jan 2012 to Present
description:
### Context 
Bitcoin is the longest running and most well known cryptocurrency, first released as open source in 2009 by the anonymous Satoshi Nakamoto. Bitcoin serves as a decentralized medium of digital exchange, with transactions verified and recorded in a public distributed ledger (the blockchain) without the need for a trusted record keeping authority or central intermediary. Transaction blocks contain a SHA-256 cryptographic hash of previous transaction blocks, and are thus "chained" together, serving as an immutable record of all transactions that have ever occurred. As with any currency/commodity on the market, bitcoin trading and financial instruments soon followed public adoption of bitcoin and continue to grow. Included here is historical bitcoin market data at 1-min intervals for select bitcoin exchanges where trading takes place. Happy (data) mining! 

### Content
(See https://github.com/mczielinski/kaggle-bitcoin/ for automation/scraping script)

```
btcusd_1-min_data.csv
```

CSV files for select bitcoin exchanges for the time period of Jan 2012 to Present (Measured by UTC day), with minute to minute updates of OHLC (Open, High, Low, Close) and Volume in BTC.

If a timestamp is missing, or if there are jumps, this may be because the exchange (or its API) was down, the exchange (or its API) did not exist, or some other unforeseen technical error in data reporting or gathering. I'm not perfect, and I'm also busy! All effort has been made to deduplicate entries and verify the contents are correct and complete to the best of my ability, but obviously trust at your own risk. 


### Acknowledgements and Inspiration

Bitcoin charts for the data, originally. Now thank you to the Bitstamp API directly. The various exchange APIs, for making it difficult or unintuitive enough to get OHLC and volume data at 1-min intervals that I set out on this data scraping project. Satoshi Nakamoto and the novel core concept of the blockchain, as well as its first execution via the bitcoin protocol. I'd also like to thank viewers like you! Can't wait to see what code or insights you all have to share.

### Agraïments

**Temporal Coverage**: 2004-01-01 to Present
**Geospatial Coverage**: Global (Gold is traded globally)

The dataset is maintained and automatically updated with the latest available data using MetaTrader 4 as the data source.

**Source Code:**

- MetaTrader 4 export script: [GitHub - MQL4 Script for fetching XAUUSD price history](https://github.com/nvn01/MQL4-Script-fetching-XAUUSD-price-history)

Feel free to use this dataset for your projects and research. If you find it useful, please consider giving it an upvote!

title: Criteo Dataset Full, Shuffled and Sampled
Hourly Crypto Market Data
subtitle: Hourly crypto data, updated hourly — unlock insights, trends & forecasts
description:
This dataset contains hourly cryptocurrency and stock market data collected from [coingecko](https://www.coingecko.com/)
 starting in January 2026. The collection pipeline was designed to demonstrate practical data management and automation skills:
- Data Ingestion: A Python Script automatically scrapes fresh hourly data from [coingecko](https://www.coingecko.com/) and writes it into Google Sheets.
- Data Offloading: To avoid Google Sheets’ row limitations, Python scripts periodically export data from Sheets into Google BigQuery.
- Data Publishing: The data is shared to Kaggle via a scheduled notebook, ensuring the dataset is updated daily with the latest available records.

This setup provides a reliable, reproducible data stream that can be used for:
- Practicing SQL queries for data extraction, filtering, and preparation before analysis
- Exploratory data analysis of crypto and stock price movements
- Building time-series forecasting models
- Studying correlations between global assets
- Demonstrating real-world ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) and data pipeline engineering

The dataset is continuously updated hourly, making it suitable both for live monitoring and historical trend analysis.

title: ArSyra Food & Culture — Arabic Culinary Dialect Da
description:
## ArSyra Food & Culture — Arabic Culinary Dialect Data

> Arabic food culture data — local dishes, ingredients, and culinary expressions.

Arabic food culture data covering local dishes, ingredients, recipes, and
food-related expressions in regional dialects. Essential for restaurant apps,
food delivery AI, and culinary NLP.

Food is deeply regional in the Arab world — the same dish has different names
across countries, and local ingredients are described in dialect. 1,000+ records
of authentic Arabic food culture data.

### Key Statistics

- **2,151** records from **17** countries
- **1** linguistic categories
- **Dialect groups:** Levantine, Maghrebi, Egyptian, Gulf, Iraqi, Sudanese, Other
- **Countries:** Syria, EU, Tunisia, Morocco, Palestine, XX, Egypt, Saudi Arabia, Algeria, Iraq, Jordan, Lebanon, UAE, Sudan, Yemen, Libya, Kuwait

### Data Format

This dataset contains structured **JSONL** files. Each record includes:
- `text` — Arabic text (dialect, MSA, or mixed)
- `category` — Linguistic category
- `country` — Speaker country code (ISO 3166-1)
- `dialect_group` — Broad dialect classification
- `quality_score` — Quality rating (0–100)
- `msa_text` — MSA equivalent (where available)

### Quick Start

```python
import json

with open('data/all.jsonl', 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    data = [json.loads(line) for line in f]

print(f'Total records: {len(data)}')
print(f'Countries: {sorted(set(r["country"] for r in data if r.get("country")))}')
print(f'Categories: {sorted(set(r["category"] for r in data if r.get("category")))}')
```

### Use Cases

- Food delivery app localization for Arabic markets
- Restaurant menu translation using dialect terms
- Culinary NLP for Arabic recipe understanding
- Food recommendation systems for Arab users

### How This Data Was Collected

Collected from verified native Arabic speakers via [ArSyra](https://arsyra.com), a multi-dialect Arabic data collection platform. All data is quality-scored, anonymized, and speakers provided informed consent.

**License:** CC-BY-NC-SA-4.0

---

### Get the Full Dataset

**This download contains a preview sample of 50 records.**

The full dataset (2,151 records) is available for instant purchase:

- **🛒 Buy Now:** [arsyra.com/datasets.html](https://arsyra.com/datasets.html)
- **Academic License:** From $29 (non-commercial use)
- **Commercial License:** From $99 (enterprise, SaaS, products)
- **Questions?** Email support@arsyra.com

Instant download as ZIP after payment. Secure checkout via Stripe.

title: 🤗 Hugging face models trending
subtitle: Daily snapshot of downloads, likes, and new releases from the HF Hub
description:
Daily-updated dataset tracking the evolution of Hugging Face model and dataset statistics.
Collected from the public HF API — no authentication required.

Each daily run appends a new snapshot row per model/dataset, enabling time-series analysis of:
- Download count growth (30-day rolling window reported by HF)
- Likes/popularity trends
- New model releases and their adoption curves
- Pipeline type distribution shifts over time
- Author/organization activity patterns

This dataset is ideal for:
- Tracking the rise and fall of popular AI models
- Identifying trending research areas (via pipeline_tag)
- Analysing the open-source AI ecosystem
- Building recommendation systems for model discovery
- Studying the HuggingFace Hub growth over time

## Column Descriptions

### hf_models_snapshot.csv / hf_models_trending.csv / hf_recent_models.csv

| Column | Type | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `snapshot_date` | date | Date of collection (YYYY-MM-DD) — use as time-series key |
| `id` | text | Full model ID (e.g., "meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B") |
| `author` | categorical | Organisation or user who published the model |
| `downloads` | numeric | 30-day download count (as reported by HF Hub) |
| `likes` | numeric | Total number of likes on HF Hub |
| `created_at` | datetime | ISO timestamp when model was first published |
| `last_modified` | datetime | ISO timestamp of last modification |
| `pipeline_tag` | categorical | Task type (e.g., "text-generation", "image-classification") |
| `library_name` | categorical | ML library (e.g., "transformers", "diffusers", "sentence-transformers") |
| `tags` | text | Pipe-separated list of all model tags |
| `private` | boolean | Whether model is private (should always be False for public API) |
| `gated` | categorical | Gating status (False, "auto", "manual") |
| `license` | categorical | SPDX license identifier (e.g., "apache-2.0", "mit") |

### hf_datasets_snapshot.csv

| Column | Type | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `snapshot_date` | date | Date of collection (YYYY-MM-DD) |
| `id` | text | Full dataset ID (e.g., "squad") |
| `author` | categorical | Organisation or user who published the dataset |
| `downloads` | numeric | 30-day download count |
| `likes` | numeric | Total number of likes |
| `created_at` | datetime | ISO timestamp of initial publication |
| `last_modified` | datetime | ISO timestamp of last modification |
| `tags` | text | Pipe-separated list of all dataset tags |
| `private` | boolean | Whether dataset is private |
| `gated` | categorical | Gating status |
| `license` | categorical | SPDX license identifier |
| `description` | text | Short description (truncated to 500 chars) |

title: Pipeline: Store Data (Dataset)
subtitle: Synthetic data drawn from a simple normal distribution, generated in a Notebook
description: This dataset is automatically updated every day through a Kaggle Notebook pipeline. Each scheduled run loads the latest dataset version, fetches one new data record via a mocked REST API, and appends it to the existing data without modifying past entries. The dataset grows incrementally over time, making it ideal for: - demonstrating daily-update data pipelines - testing incremental data processing workflows - experimenting with reproducible and automated dataset generation - showcasing how to build a continuous data stream on Kaggle All data is synthetic and generated within the pipeline, ensuring full reproducibility. ## Pipeline Navigation - **Pipeline: Create Data (Notebook)** https://www.kaggle.com/code/hideos/pipeline-create-data-notebook - **Pipeline: Store Data (Dataset)** https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/hideos/synthetic-sample-dataset-generated-via-notebook - **Pipeline: Use Data (Notebook)** https://www.kaggle.com/code/hideos/pipeline-use-data-notebook ## Discussion https://www.kaggle.com/discussions/general/674168 If you found this pipeline useful, feel free to join the discussion! Photo by Mathew Schwartz on Unsplash

title: Indic NLP Library
subtitle: Indic NLP Library is  for common text processing and NLP for Indian languages
description:
Special thanks to [Anoop Kunchukuttan](https://github.com/anoopkunchukuttan) for making this wonderful package.
Source: https://github.com/rwightman/pytorch-image-models
Installation: see this [notebook](https://nbviewer.org/url/anoopkunchukuttan.github.io/indic_nlp_library/doc/indic_nlp_examples.ipynb) for quick tutorial
```
import sys
sys.path.append('../input/indic-nlp-library')
import  indicnlp
```

title: Use of technology by university students
subtitle: Use of technology by university students with online modality
description: It is a qualitative survey on the use of technology in university students with studies in the online modality. The survey seeks to understand the use that university students make of technology in their daily lives and how it influences their learning process.

title: israeli-supermarkets-data
subtitle: A versioned daily dump of the data published by the Israeli supermarkets chain
description:
We are looking for contributors! contact me at erlichsefi@gmail.com
 
This dataset is being maintained by an automatic process defined in the open-source [Github organization](https://github.com/orgs/OpenIsraeliSupermarkets/repositories).
The data dumped here is raw data, scraped incrementally every 4 hours, and pushed here at midnight. 
For each chain,  you can find:
 - [REPLACE_WITH_CHAIN_NAME].json : which describe the scraper execution
 - price_file_[REPLACE_WITH_CHAIN_NAME].csv : which is all files indentified as price update files.
 - price_full_file_[REPLACE_WITH_CHAIN_NAME].csv : which is all files indentified as full snapshot of the price files.
 - promo_file_[REPLACE_WITH_CHAIN_NAME].csv : which is all files indentified as prompo update files.
 - promo_full_file_[REPLACE_WITH_CHAIN_NAME].csv : which is all files indentified as full snapshot of the promo files.
 - stores_[REPLACE_WITH_CHAIN_NAME].csv : which is all files indentified as full snapshot of the store files.



IMPORTANT!
Up Until 14/12/2025, there was an issue in the parsing that caused only the last 'ItemCode' in a list of ItemCodes under a single promotion from being published.

title: PulmoBench
subtitle: A Structured Clinical Reasoning Benchmark for lung cancer Pulmonology
description:
# Overview

PulmoBench is a structured benchmark designed to evaluate large language models in clinical risk stratification and safety-aware pulmonary reasoning.

The dataset is derived from a publicly available lung cancer risk dataset and transformed into natural language clinical vignettes with structured annotations.

## Dataset Structure

Each sample contains:

- case_id (int)
- age (int)
- sex (str)
- risk_tier (low / medium / high)
- risk_score (0–2 ordinal)
- urgency (routine_followup / specialist_consult / urgent_ct_scan / immediate_referral)
- gold_differential (list[str])
- escalation_required (bool)
- vignette (str)
- perturbation_type (original / lexical_rephrase / symptom_removed / contradictory_signal)

## Task Definition

Given a vignette, models must predict:

- Risk tier
- Urgency
- Differential diagnoses
- Safety escalation

## Intended Use

PulmoBench is designed for evaluating:

Clinical reasoning robustness

Prompt engineering sensitivity

Safety compliance

Escalation reliability

Not intended for real-world medical deployment.

### Source Dataset

Derived from:
"Cancer Patients and Air Pollution: A New Link"
(Include citation + license)

title: Horse Racing results - UK/Ireland 2015 - 2026
subtitle: a frequently updated sqlite3 database / csv file **UPDATED to 23rd Feb 2026
description:
`raceform.db is a sqlie3 database with schema as follows:

CREATE TABLE data (
    date        NUMERIC,
    course      TEXT,
    race_id     INTEGER,
    off         TEXT,
    race_name   TEXT,
    type        TEXT,
    class       TEXT,
    pattern     TEXT,
    rating_band TEXT,
    age_band    TEXT,
    sex_rest    TEXT,
    dist        TEXT,
    going       TEXT,
    ran         INTEGER,
    num         INTEGER,
    pos         INTEGER,
    draw        INTEGER,
    ovr_btn     NUMERIC,
    btn         NUMERIC,
    horse       TEXT,
    age         INTEGER,
    sex         TEXT,
    wgt         TEXT,
    hg          TEXT,
    time        TEXT,
    sp          TEXT,
    jockey      TEXT,
    trainer     TEXT,
    prize       INTEGER,
    [or]        INTEGER,
    rpr         INTEGER,
    ts          INTEGER,
    sire        TEXT,
    dam         TEXT,
    damsire     TEXT,
    owner       TEXT,
    comment     TEXT
);

title: Operation_Profile_Carriers
subtitle: container vessel, Capital Expenditures for upgrading new energy system.
description:
Operation Profile for bulk carriers and container vessel, Capital Expenditures for upgrading new energy system at newbuilding stage for Newcastle bulk carrier and Mega container vessel respectively.
All the data are operational data from a Mega container ship and a Newcastle bulk carrier, kindly shared by two friendly ship owners, with the commitment to well protect owners’s interest. Those data are used to calculate the fuel consumption and carbon emissions during ship operation. Based on those data, the ship’s CII rating can be analyzed, as well as the calculations of ETS and FuelEU under the EU emission reduction framework, providing a data foundation for the future analysis of the IMO Net-Zero Strategy. Meanwhile, the data provide the CAPEX for Mega container ships and Newcastle bulk carriers using different alternative fuels, including conventional fuel, LNG, methanol, and ammonia, together with the initial investment for scrubbers, based on market conditions and years of shipbuilding experience, thus supporting the economic analysis of different fuel options.
In 2025, the company contracted Hengli Shipbuilding for the construction of a 181,000-dwt scrubber-fitted Capesize vessel scheduled for delivery in Q2 2027. The owner has now secured a sister vessel of similar specifications, slated for delivery in Q3 2027, at a contract price of approximately US$75M.

title: The Sweet Side of Data: B.Laban Customer Behavior
subtitle: Master Data Science with 10k simulated B.Laban transactions & 20+ SQL expert
description:
Overview
This dataset provides a comprehensive simulation of B.Laban (ب.لبن) sales transactions across multiple regions including Egypt, UAE, and KSA. It is designed for data scientists to practice Data Cleaning, Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA), and Machine Learning regression models to predict total sales.

The data is intentionally "messy" to mimic real-world scenarios, containing missing values (NAs), extreme outliers, and inconsistent entries.

📂 Dataset Structure
The project is divided into three main files:

blaban_train_v2.csv: 7,000 records including the target variable (Total_Sales).

blaban_test_v2.csv: 3,000 records without the target variable for model testing.

blaban_submission_v2.csv: A sample submission format with Transaction_ID and Predicted_Sales.

📊 Key Features (28 Columns)
Transaction Info: Transaction_ID, Date_Time, Order_Source.

Location: Branch, Region.

Product Details: Product_Name, Category, Size, Topping_Type.

Financials: Unit_Price, Quantity, Discount_Rate, Tax_Amount, Total_Sales.

Customer Insights: Customer_Age, Customer_Gender, Membership_Status.

External Factors: Temperature_Celsius, Humidity_Percent, Is_Public_Holiday, Is_Weekend.

🛠️ Challenges for Users
Outlier Detection: Handle extreme prices (e.g., 9999 EGP for a dessert).

Missing Data: Handle NaN values in Customer_Age and Store_Rating.

Feature Engineering: Create new insights from Date_Time and weather conditions.

title: Capital Weather Data - 1995-2024
subtitle: Daily weather statistics for every world capital 1995 to 2024 (via Open-Meteo)
description:
# 🌍 Global Major Cities Daily Weather (1995 – 2024) [CURRENTLY UNDER CONSTRUCTION]

This dataset provides **daily historical weather data** for **major cities worldwide**, combining reliable climate reanalysis from the **[Open-Meteo Historical API](https://open-meteo.com/)** with geographic and population data from **[Wikidata](https://www.wikidata.org/)**.

It includes all **national capitals** and the largest population centers in every country, giving a detailed global view of urban climate from **1995 to 2024**.

---

## 📦 Files

| File | Description |
|------|--------------|
| `cities_clean.parquet` | Metadata for all selected cities (country, ISO code, lat/lon, population, capital flag). |
| `history.parquet` | Complete daily weather records (one row per city × date). |
| `history_latest.csv` | Snapshot of the most recent day available. |

---

## 🌆 City Coverage

“Major cities” are defined using a reproducible Wikidata query that returns all entities of type *city* (`Q515`) with coordinates.  
Cities are included if they meet at least one of the following:

- Are a **national capital**, or  
- Have a **population ≥ 300 000**, or  
- Rank among the **10 most populous** cities in their country.

If a live query to Wikidata fails, the generator automatically **falls back** to a previously saved `cities_clean.parquet` file from a prior run or from the dataset input, ensuring continuity between updates.

---

## ☀️ Weather Variables

Daily statistics from Open-Meteo’s ERA5-based reanalysis include:

| Variable | Unit | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `temp_max_c`, `temp_min_c` | °C | Maximum and minimum 2 m temperature |
| `temp_mean_c_approx` | °C | Approximate daily mean ((max + min)/2) |
| `app_temp_max_c`, `app_temp_min_c` | °C | Apparent (feels-like) temperature |
| `precip_mm`, `rain_mm`, `snow_mm` | mm | Total daily precipitation, rain, and snowfall |
| `windspeed_10m_max_kmh`, `windgusts_10m_max_kmh` | km/h | Maximum windspeed and gusts |
| `wind_dir_dom_deg` | ° | Dominant wind direction |
| `sunshine_duration_s`, `daylight_duration_s` | s | Total daily sunshine and daylight duration |
| `shortwave_radiation_MJ_m2` | MJ/m² | Daily solar shortwave radiation sum |

All values are daily aggregates in **UTC**.

---

## ⚙️ Data Collection & Processing

- Coordinates and population metadata are fetched via Wikidata’s public SPARQL endpoint.  
- Historical weather is pulled year-by-year from the Open-Meteo archive.  
- The script checks for existing yearly records per city to **avoid redundant API calls**.  
- Robust retry logic handles rate limits and network errors.  
- After each batch of merges, a new version of `history.parquet` is saved for incremental backup.  
- If five consecutive 429 (“Too Many Requests”) responses occur, the run pauses gracefully and resumes automatically in the next scheduled execution.

---

## ⚖️ License & Attribution

Data is released under **CC BY 4.0**. Please credit:

title: ByT5 Akkadian Scratch SentAlign
subtitle: ByT5-base trained from scratch on sentence-aligned Akkadian-English data
description: Trained from google/byt5-base on 9,650 sentence-aligned pairs extracted from train.csv + published_texts.csv + Sentences_Oare. Training: FP32, lr=5e-5, batch=16, cosine schedule on A100.

title: The Enron Email Dataset📩📪
subtitle: 📪Spam Mail Prediction | Machine Leaning
description:
The Enron email dataset contains approximately 500,000 emails generated by employees of the Enron Corporation. It was obtained by the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission during its investigation of Enron's collapse.

This is the May 7, 2015 Version of dataset, as published at https://www.cs.cmu.edu/~./enron/

title: BLDG-SIM Building Simulation Email List Text
subtitle: 1999-2019 Corpus of Emails from a large building simulation community email list
description:
## This dataset and the explanation below are from the 2019 IBPSA Paper: [Twenty years of building simulation trends: Text mining and topic modeling of the Bldg-sim email list archive](http://www.ibpsa.org/proceedings/BS2019/BS2019_211087.pdf)

### Context

The recent growth of the building performance re- search community has been in parallel with the [onebuilding.org Bldg-sim email list](http://lists.onebuilding.org/listinfo.cgi/bldg-sim-onebuilding.org). This list was formed in 1999 and steadily grew into a major venue for building simulation community announcements, dis- cussions, and questions and answers (Q&A). This pa- per presents an analysis of the Bldg-sim email archive to determine how traffic has grown over the years and what general trends have come and gone in this par- ticular user community.

### Content

We use a text mining ap- proach to find the most prominent topics over the years and create trend metrics from the frequency of the most common words from those topics. The results illustrate the relative rise and fall of various soft- ware tools, organizations, and simulation topics from the portion of the simulation community who used the email list. Visualizations showing the trends are presented and discussed. The paper also discusses the generalizability of results as it should be noted that the users of this email list are primarily English- speaking simulation practitioners from North Amer- ica. All data and code used in this analysis are avail- able in a reproducible GitHub repository.

### Acknowledgements

This paper and analysis is only possible with the help of Jason Glazer, the founder of the list serv

### Inspiration

Slice and dice the email list content to find trends and interesting insights about the building simulation community

[Banner image source](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Email)

title: COVID-19 Coronavirus data - weekly
subtitle: COVID-19 Coronavirus data - weekly (from 17 December 2020)
description:
### Content

The dataset contains a weekly situation update on COVID-19, the epidemiological curve and the global geographical distribution (EU/EEA and the UK, worldwide).

Since the beginning of the coronavirus pandemic, ECDC’s Epidemic Intelligence team has collected the number of COVID-19 cases and deaths, based on reports from health authorities worldwide. This comprehensive and systematic process was carried out on a daily basis until 14/12/2020. See the discontinued daily dataset: COVID-19 Coronavirus data - daily. ECDC’s decision to discontinue daily data collection is based on the fact that the daily number of cases reported or published by countries is frequently subject to retrospective corrections, delays in reporting and/or clustered reporting of data for several days. Therefore, the daily number of cases may not reflect the true number of cases at EU/EEA level at a given day of reporting. Consequently, day to day variations in the number of cases does not constitute a valid basis for policy decisions.

ECDC continues to monitor the situation. Every week between Monday and Wednesday, a team of epidemiologists screen up to 500 relevant sources to collect the latest figures for publication on Thursday. The data screening is followed by ECDC’s standard epidemic intelligence process for which every single data entry is validated and documented in an ECDC database. An extract of this database, complete with up-to-date figures and data visualisations, is then shared on the ECDC website, ensuring a maximum level of transparency.

ECDC receives regular updates from EU/EEA countries through the Early Warning and Response System (EWRS), The European Surveillance System (TESSy), the World Health Organization (WHO) and email exchanges with other international stakeholders. This information is complemented by screening up to 500 sources every day to collect COVID-19 figures from 196 countries. This includes websites of ministries of health (43% of the total number of sources), websites of public health institutes (9%), websites from other national authorities (ministries of social services and welfare, governments, prime minister cabinets, cabinets of ministries, websites on health statistics and official response teams) (6%), WHO websites and WHO situation reports (2%), and official dashboards and interactive maps from national and international institutions (10%). In addition, ECDC screens social media accounts maintained by national authorities on for example Twitter, Facebook, YouTube or Telegram accounts run by ministries of health (28%) and other official sources (e.g. official media outlets) (2%). Several media and social media sources are screened to gather additional information which can be validated with the official sources previously mentioned. Only cases and deaths reported by the national and regional competent authorities from the countries and territories listed are aggregated in our database.

Disclaimer: National updates are published at different times and in different time zones. This, and the time ECDC needs to process these data, might lead to discrepancies between the national numbers and the numbers published by ECDC. Users are advised to use all data with caution and awareness of their limitations. Data are subject to retrospective corrections; corrected datasets are released as soon as processing of updated national data has been completed. 

### Source 
- https://data.europa.eu/euodp/en/data/dataset/covid-19-coronavirus-data-weekly-from-17-december-2020

title: imagecodecs-offline
subtitle: Offline Wheel for imagecodecs (Python 3.10/3.11 Linux)
description: This dataset provides the .whl file for imagecodecs, specifically built for Linux environments like Kaggle. This is essential for competitions like the Vesuvius Challenge where you need to read compressed .tif files while the internet is disabled during submission.

title: Top Spotify Podcast Episodes (Daily Updated)
subtitle: Top 200 Spotify Podcast Episodes in 22 countries
description:
This dataset contains daily snapshot of Spotify's top 200 podcast episodes for every country. It also includes detailed information about podcast episodes and shows from Spotify API.

`DAILY DATA COLLECTION STARTED ON 2025-10-11`

There is also some historical data:
2024-09-02 - 2024-10-23

`Please upvote if you found it useful 😃`

By analyzing this data, you can uncover insights such as:

- Trending Podcasts: Discover the most popular podcast episodes in different regions.
- Cultural Preferences: Compare podcast popularity to understand cultural influences on podcast consumption.
- Trend Analysis: Track how podcast rankings change over time to identify emerging trends.
- Content Analysis: Dive into episode metadata for sentiment analysis, topic modeling, or genre classification.


**Spotify Regions:**
Argentina 🇦🇷 ("ar")
Australia 🇦🇺 ("au")
Austria 🇦🇹 ("at")
Brazil 🇧🇷 ("br")
Canada 🇨🇦 ("ca")
Chile 🇨🇱 ("cl")
Colombia 🇨🇴 ("co")
France 🇫🇷 ("fr")
Germany 🇩🇪 ("de")
India 🇮🇳 ("in")
Indonesia 🇮🇩 ("id")
Ireland 🇮🇪 ("ie")
Italy 🇮🇹 ("it")
Japan 🇯🇵 ("jp")
Mexico 🇲🇽 ("mx")
New Zealand 🇳🇿 ("nz")
Philippines 🇵🇭 ("ph")
Poland 🇵🇱 ("pl")
Spain 🇪🇸 ("es")
Netherlands 🇳🇱 ("nl")
United Kingdom 🇬🇧 ("gb")
United States 🇺🇸 ("us")

Source: https://podcastcharts.byspotify.com/

Spotify API used to enrich the source: https://developer.spotify.com/documentation/web-api/reference/get-an-episode

Code: https://github.com/Woys/Spotify-Podcasts-Airflow-Batch
